steps.title: Apply the theorem
steps.content: The given side lengths, $32$ and $28$, are the legs of a right triangle. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the hypotenuse $c$.

steps.title: Factor before simplifying
steps.content:
Both legs are divisible by $4$, so factor that out before taking the square root.
$$c^2=32^2+28^2$$
$$c^2=[4(8)]^2+[4(7)]^2$$
$$c^2=16(64)+16(49)$$
$$c^2=16(113)$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical
steps.content: Take the square root of both sides. $$c=\sqrt{16(113)}=4\sqrt{113}$$ The answer is B.
